title: Keap Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Keap** (formerly Infusionsoft) is a small-business CRM + marketing-automation platform exposing a REST API under `https://api.infusionsoft.com/crm/rest/v1`. This connector — `KeapConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "keap"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Keap account:

| Surface | Path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Contacts | `/contacts` | List, get, create, update, delete contacts |
| Opportunities | `/opportunities` | List + create sales opportunities |
| Orders | `/orders` | List e-commerce orders |
| Tags | `/tags`, `/tags/{id}/contacts` | List tags, apply / remove on contacts |
| Campaigns | `/campaigns/{id}/sequences/{sid}/contacts/{cid}` | List campaigns, enroll contacts in sequences |
| Emails | `/emails/queue` | Send transactional emails |
| Account | `/account/profile` | Health-check probe |

The connector normalises contacts + opportunities + orders into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), refreshes OAuth2 access tokens transparently on 401 via a one-shot HTTP-client callback, retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (Retry-After honoured), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::KeapHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Register an OAuth2 application in Keap

1. Sign in to https://accounts.infusionsoft.com.
2. Open the **Developer Portal** (https://keys.developer.keap.com).
3. Click **Create App**, name it (e.g. *Shielva*).
4. Add the **Redirect URI** that the Shielva gateway will use (it's shown next to the install fields in ACP).
5. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** values from the *Keys* tab.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Keap**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (text, required) | OAuth2 Client ID from step 5 |
| `client_secret` (secret, required) | OAuth2 Client Secret from step 5 |
| `redirect_uri` (text, optional) | Gateway injects when blank |
| `scopes` (text, optional, default `full`) | Keap only exposes the `full` scope today |
| `authorization_url` (text, optional) | Defaults to `https://accounts.infusionsoft.com/app/oauth/authorize` |
| `token_url` (text, optional) | Defaults to `https://api.infusionsoft.com/token` |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Defaults to `https://api.infusionsoft.com/crm/rest/v1` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Default 60 (Keap-published cap) |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `client_id` + `client_secret` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `KeapHTTPClient`, and returns `InstallResult(success=True, auth_status=CONNECTED)`.

### 3. Complete OAuth

The gateway redirects the user to `get_authorization_url(...)`. After Keap calls back with `?code=...`, the gateway invokes `authorize(auth_code, state)` which POSTs `grant_type=authorization_code` to the token URL and stores the resulting `TokenInfo`.

### 4. First health check

`health_check()` calls `GET /account/profile`. 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. 401 → `UNHEALTHY + FAILED`. 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Keap uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code grant** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) with mandatory refresh tokens.

### Token lifecycle

- Access tokens expire after ~24 h (`expires_in` is honoured per the response).
- Refresh tokens are single-use; Keap rotates them on every refresh. `on_token_refresh()` stores the rotated value via `set_token`.
- `is_token_valid()` compares `_token_info.expires_at` against `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (tz-aware) — all expiries are stored tz-aware.

### Header contract

Every outbound request to `https://api.infusionsoft.com/crm/rest/v1/*`:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

### Transparent refresh on 401

`KeapHTTPClient._send` calls the connector-supplied `token_refresher` callback exactly once per request. The callback runs `_refresh_access_token → on_token_refresh → set_token` and returns the new bearer string; the request is retried with the new token. Repeated 401 after refresh surfaces as `KeapAuthError`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | text | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth2 Client ID |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth2 Client Secret |
| `redirect_uri` | text | no | `self.redirect_uri` | OAuth callback URL |
| `scopes` | text | no | `self.scopes` (default `"full"`) | Space-separated OAuth scopes |
| `authorization_url` | text | no | `self.auth_url` | Override only if Keap moves the endpoint |
| `token_url` | text | no | `self.token_url` | Override only if Keap moves the endpoint |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` | Override for v2 or proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "keap"
AUTH_TYPE      = "oauth2_code"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
REQUIRED_SCOPES      = ["full"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: (DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED),
    403: (UNHEALTHY, FAILED),
    404: (HEALTHY,   CONNECTED),
    429: (DEGRADED,  CONNECTED),
    500: (OFFLINE,   CONNECTED),
}
AUTH_URI  = "https://accounts.infusionsoft.com/app/oauth/authorize"
TOKEN_URI = "https://api.infusionsoft.com/token"
_KEAP_BASE = "https://api.infusionsoft.com/crm/rest/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `KeapConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::KeapHTTPClient`; normalization lives in `helpers/normalizer.py`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> InstallResult`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`. Persist via `save_config`. Does NOT call the API. Returns `InstallResult(success=True/False, auth_status=CONNECTED|MISSING_CREDENTIALS)`.

### `get_authorization_url(redirect_uri=None, state=None) -> str`
Build the OAuth2 consent URL — used by the gateway to redirect the user.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state=None) -> TokenInfo`
POST `grant_type=authorization_code` to `token_url`. Persist via `set_token`.

### `async on_token_refresh() -> TokenInfo`
POST `grant_type=refresh_token` to `token_url`. Returns rotated `TokenInfo`.

### `async health_check() -> HealthCheckResult`
`GET /account/profile`. Maps failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages through contacts → `normalize_contact` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with counts.

children.title: Contacts
children.content:
### `async list_contacts(limit=50, offset=0, email=None, given_name=None, family_name=None) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts` — offset/limit pagination; optional Keap query filters.

### `async get_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`GET /contacts/{id}`.

### `async create_contact(given_name=None, family_name=None, email_addresses=None, phone_numbers=None) -> Dict`
`POST /contacts`. `email_addresses` follows Keap's `[{email, field: 'EMAIL1'}]` shape; `phone_numbers` similarly `[{number, field: 'PHONE1'}]`.

### `async update_contact(contact_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /contacts/{id}` — partial update.

### `async delete_contact(contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /contacts/{id}`.

children.title: Opportunities + Orders
children.content:
### `async list_opportunities(limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /opportunities`.

### `async create_opportunity(opportunity_title, contact_id, stage_id, projected_revenue=0) -> Dict`
`POST /opportunities` — body: `{opportunity_title, contact: {id}, stage: {id}, projected_revenue}`.

### `async list_orders(limit=50, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /orders`.

children.title: Tags + Campaigns + Emails
children.content:
### `async list_tags() -> Dict`
`GET /tags`.

### `async apply_tag(tag_id, contact_ids) -> Dict`
`POST /tags/{tag_id}/contacts` — body: `{ids: [...]}`.

### `async remove_tag(tag_id, contact_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /tags/{tag_id}/contacts/{contact_id}`.

### `async list_campaigns(limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /campaigns`.

### `async add_contact_to_campaign(campaign_id, sequence_id, contact_id) -> Dict`
`POST /campaigns/{cid}/sequences/{sid}/contacts/{contact_id}`.

### `async send_email(contact_ids, subject, html_content='', plain_content='') -> Dict`
`POST /emails/queue` — body: `{contacts: [...], subject, html_content, plain_content}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
KeapError                 # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── KeapAuthError         # 401 / 403
├── KeapNotFound          # 404 — resource + resource_id
├── KeapRateLimitError    # 429 — retry_after seconds
└── KeapNetworkError      # transient transport / 5xx
```

### Retry behaviour (`helpers/utils.py::with_retry`)

| Exception | Action |
|---|---|
| `KeapRateLimitError` | Honour `Retry-After` on first attempt; exponential backoff thereafter (`base * 2 ** attempt` + jitter, capped at `MAX_RETRY_DELAY_S=32`); retry up to `max_retries` (default 3) |
| `KeapNetworkError` | Same backoff; retry up to `max_retries` |
| `httpx.TransportError` | Same backoff; retry up to `max_retries` |
| `KeapAuthError` | Surface immediately to caller (the HTTP client already attempted a one-shot refresh) |
| `KeapNotFound` | Surface immediately |

### Transparent OAuth refresh on 401

When the HTTP layer sees a 401 and a `token_refresher` is installed, it calls the refresher exactly once, retries the in-flight request with the new bearer, and only surfaces `KeapAuthError` if the retry also fails. This keeps token-lifecycle logic out of every business method.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
200 → HealthCheckResult(healthy=True,  health=HEALTHY,   auth=CONNECTED)
401 → HealthCheckResult(healthy=False, health=UNHEALTHY, auth=FAILED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `InstallResult(auth_status=MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Both fields are required; the gateway can inject the redirect URI but never the credentials. |
| `KeapAuthError` immediately after OAuth callback | `redirect_uri` mismatch between Keap app and ACP | Verify the redirect URI registered in the Keap Developer Portal **exactly** matches what the gateway sends (trailing slash, scheme). |
| `KeapAuthError: Refresh failed` | Refresh token revoked or expired (Keap rotates aggressively) | Re-run the OAuth consent flow from ACP → Reconnect; the new authorize call mints a fresh refresh token. |
| `KeapRateLimitError` storms | Hitting Keap's 60 req/min cap on bulk sync | Lower `rate_limit_per_min`, increase backoff `BACKOFF_FACTOR` in `helpers/utils.py`, or batch fewer requests. The client already honours `Retry-After`. |
| `KeapNotFound` on `get_contact(id)` | Contact deleted, or wrong tenant | Verify the contact still exists in Keap. Remember `NormalizedDocument.id = f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"` — never lookup across tenants. |
| `TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes` | Test fixture seeded `expires_at` with `datetime.utcnow()` instead of `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` | Use `datetime.now(timezone.utc)` everywhere — `BaseConnector.is_token_valid()` compares tz-aware. The shipped `_seed_token` test helper is correct. |
| Health-check passes but `list_contacts` 401s | The HTTP-client `token_refresher` was disabled (e.g. via `set_token_refresher(None)` in a test) | Re-install the refresher: `conn.http_client.set_token_refresher(conn._refresh_access_token)`. |
| OAuth code exchange returns `invalid_grant` | Authorization code already exchanged or expired (Keap codes live ~30 s) | Re-run the consent flow from ACP — each code is single-use. |
